title: Matt Cornett Shares the Advice He Got From Old Roommate, ‘The Summer I Turned Pretty’s’ Gavin Casalegno
summary: When Matt Cornett booked Prime Video’s “Every Year After,” he knew just who to call: his old roommate and “The Summer I Turned Pretty” star Gavin Casalegno.
story: When Matt Cornett booked the lead role in Prime Video’s “Every Year After,” he knew just who to call: his old roommate.

“Every Year After” is the latest Prime Video book-to-screen adaptation. The romance follows childhood friends Sam Florek (Cornett) and Percy Fraser (Sadie Soverall) as they spent their summers together in Barry’s Bay, Ontario. The eight-episode series is adapted from the 2022 bestselling novel by Carley Fortune, titled “Every Summer After.”

While appearing on TODAY June 10 alongside Soverall, Cornett shared that he used to room with actor Gavin Casalegno, known for his role as Jeremiah Fisher in “The Summer I Turned Pretty” — another Prime Video book-to-screen adaptation.
“Gavin and I have been friends for quite a long time,” Cornett said on TODAY June 10. “We lived together before he got ‘The Summer I Turned Pretty.’
“When I told him I was doing this show, we actually were at lunch one day,” Cornett added. “I was like, ‘Yeah, I’m doing this next Amazon book adaptation, summer show.’”
Casalegno immediately offered some advice.
“He was like, ‘Get ready, the book fans are incredible, and they are intense,’” Cornett recalled.
He added that he loves the fan dedication.
“They know their books, and I think they know Sam better than me, which is kind of scary sometimes,” he said.
“Means you are doing it right,” co-host Craig Melvin chimed in.

“Every Year After” was given a series order in 2024, and the cast was announced in July 2025. Alongside Fortune, the series is backed by showrunner and executive producer Amy B. Harris, who came into the project with two previous book-to-screen experiences on her resume: “The Carrie Diaries and “Sex and the City.”
All eight episodes are available to stream — or, more likely, binge —  now on Prime Video.

title: Katy Perry, Michael Bublé and More Stars Performing at the 2026 World Cup Opening Ceremonies
summary: The FIFA 2026 World Cup will be a star-studded event, bringing soccer players and entertainers like Katy Perry, Belinda, J Balvin, Maná, Alanis Morissette and more together.
story: The FIFA 2026 World Cup is set to be one star-studded event, bringing soccer players and entertainers together for a global celebration.

The worldwide tournament officially kicks off on Thursday, June 11, with the Mexico vs. South Africa game in Mexico City. Before the match starts, fans will be treated to an exciting opening ceremony that will include Shakira and Burna Boy singing the FIFA World Cup official song, “Dai Dai,” for the first time.
Other stars will also join in on the 90-minute opener. And that’s not all. There will be two more ceremonies, one in Canada and another in the U.S., on Friday, June 12.
This year’s World Cup is being hosted by the three North American countries, each with their own opening ceremony and native singers to celebrate the global event.
Here is everything you need to know, including all the singers who will be performing.
When Are the World Cup Opening Ceremonies?
The first FIFA World Cup opening ceremony will take place before the Mexico vs. South Africa match that starts at 12 p.m. PT/3 p.m. ET on Thursday, June 11.
The second will be held on Friday, June 12, before the Canada vs. Bosnia and Herzegovina match that begins at 12 p.m. PT/3 p.m. ET.
The U.S. will have its opening ceremony that same day. It will start 90-minutes before their game against Paraguay, which kicks off at 6 p.m. PT/9 p.m. ET.
Who’s Performing at the Opening Ceremony in Mexico?

Shakira and Burna Boy will perform “Dai Dai” for the first time during the Mexico opening ceremony. Also taking the stage before the match starts at Mexico City Stadium are Alejandro Fernández, Belinda, Danny Ocean, J Balvin, Lila Downs, Los Ángeles Azules and Maná.
Who’s Performing at the Opening Ceremony in Canada?

For Canada’s opening ceremony, Alanis Morissette, Alessia Cara, Elyanna, Jessie Reyez, Michael Bublé, Nora Fatehi, Sanjoy, Vegedream, William Prince and more artists will take the stage at the BMO Field in Toronto.
Who’s Performing at the Opening Ceremony in the US?
Last but not least, the U.S. will hold its opening ceremony in Los Angeles at SoFi Stadium. Katy Perry, Future, Anitta, LISA, Rema and Tyla are all set to perform.
Additionally, Dan + Shay will be on hand to sing the national anthem, while Purahei Soul will perform the Paraguay national anthem.

title: ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8 Power Rankings: Week 1’s Winners, Losers and Surprises
summary: TODAY.com’s “Love Island USA” Season 8 commentators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr Griffin discuss the first fan vote of the season.
story: In TODAY.com’s new weekly “Love Island USA” column, content creators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr Griffin break down the latest group of Season 8 episodes, sharing their thoughts on the new bombshells, couplings and dumping. Check back here over the next six weeks to read the duo’s hot takes and follow @tessdhiggins and @sallydarrgriffin on their socials to keep up with them throughout the week.
It has been a messy few days in the “Love Island USA” villa.
Season 8 of the Peacock reality dating series has already delivered jaw-dropping challenges, six bold bombshells and one emotional elimination in just a week.

In Episode 5, Sean became the first islander to be dumped from the villa. Then, Episode 6 featured a new challenge that involved a giant bed, blindfolds, handcuffs, paint and three bombshells. The arrival of Caleb, Jen and Sol brought tests for multiple couples, including one pair that TODAY.com’s “Love Island USA” Season 8 commentators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr both labeled as their “Best Couple” after the premiere.
By the end of Episode 7 the following couples remained in the villa…
Trinity and Bryce
Trinity and Bryce
Melanie and Sincere
Melanie and Sincere
Kenzie and Corbin
Kenzie and Corbin
Aniya and KC
Aniya and KC
Kayda and Zach
Kayda and Zach
Beatriz and Gabriel
Beatriz and Gabriel

But those pairs might not last for long as the episode concluded with narrator Iain Stirling announcing the first fan vote of the season. Viewers can vote via the app on the islander they want Caleb, Jen and Sol to couple up with. The results will be shown on the next episode, out Thursday, June 11.
How do Sally Darr and Tess think the vote will play out?
Sally Darr says Melanie, Corbin and Bea could find themselves single and vulnerable, and she isn’t sure who would be dumped between the three of them.

“There’s kind of two schools of thought with this,” she explains. “Do you keep the people who are building connections or do you keep the people who need more time? In previous years, specifically in the last season, I feel like the islanders were always keeping people who “needed more time” or who just got here.

As for Tess, she says Melanie’s spot in the villa could be in jeopardy. “But I don’t think that they’re going to want to get rid of Melanie so soon, because that would leave kind of an empty drama gap,” she adds.
Read on for more of their thoughts about the latest episodes, including the unraveling of one fan-favorite pair, below.
Best Moment

Tess: “I loved when we got to see Bryce giving Trinity the tea on how Melanie was going back and forth between kissing Corbin and Sincere. It really just highlighted their playful dynamic. I think it was like a really memeable moment, too, just to see them laughing and going back and forth gossiping about what was going on.”

Sally Darr: “Melanie denying that she knew who she was kissing in the paint challenge was cracking me up because she definitely knew. Specifically when KC was talking to her and he was saying, ‘Melanie, you know I love you. Let’s be honest here.’ And she just continued to deny it. Bryce and Trinity were also joking in their bed. Trinity was saying, ‘You know she was blindfolded. Maybe she genuinely didn’t know.’ Bryce was saying ‘Trinity, who are we kidding here?’ They were giggling and laughing. I just thought that her continuing to deny it was so funny. Then on top of that, Sincere kissing Sol in the “Say Less” area, and later the Speakeasy area, but we’ll get to that. Sincere kissing Sol was a really pointed response to how Melanie has been moving and I’m here for the drama. You know that whole situation is just giving us really good TV.”
Biggest Surprise

Tess: “Sol and Sincere’s kiss because I didn’t expect him to go for it right away, and the fact that Melanie almost walked in on them was timed out so perfectly for maximum impact. I really think it’s going to impact the way that the audience votes, being left with that as the last thing we see at the end of the episode.”

Sally Darr: “I would say Sincere not telling Melanie that he kissed Sol twice. He told her that he kissed Sol in the Speakeasy area, but he did not tell her that he kissed her earlier in the “Say Less” area. I just think that that could come back to hurt him, not giving her the full truth. We know that the ‘Love Island’ producers can use that kind of stuff as ammo on something like “Movie Night.” I think it’s fine that they kissed but I think we’ve gotten to know Melanie. I don’t think she’s going to be happy with not having the full truth. I think that was really naive of him.”
Best Couple

Tess: “Aniya and KC. I really like how they’re being portrayed so far. We’re seeing a lot of depth to their connection, especially in that conversation they had on the dock in this episode, where Aniya was expressing how she was upset about what happened during the challenge. (It) kind of felt like he understood what she meant and was going to take accountability and try to right the wrongs.”

Sally Darr: “Bryce and Trinity are cracking me up. I don’t know if it’s just a friendship thing, if it’s going to grow into more, but they seem to have good banter. They’re giggling, they’re having fun. I also love KC and Aniya. I am a tiny bit worried that KC’s head is going to turn because he is kind of interested in Sol. But he did reassure Aniya so I hope that they’re good. I hope that they stay together. I think that they’re really sweet.”
Biggest Winner

Tess: “Trinity and Bryce as a couple, just because they have so little drama and are just having fun right now. Everyone else has conflict in their couple to some degree, which makes Trinity and Bryce’s scenes more easy to watch and more fun.”

Sally Darr: “All of the bombshells have the advantage right now. I would say that Sol specifically is crushing it. She’s really shaken things up and I think that she’s showing the audience that she isn’t just going to go for the easy option. She kissed Gabriel and she said that she wasn’t interested, so she’s not just going to take attention wherever she can get it. She’s coming across as really genuine and she’s really interested in Sincere. So she’s kind of breaking up a couple that’s been together since day one. If she’s going to be hanging out with someone and kissing someone, it’s going to be because she’s actually into it. I think she’s kind of building a trust with the audience in a way. She’s the winner tonight.”
At Risk of Being Dumped

Tess: “Melanie. I think she’s the most vulnerable. Because if the votes go the way I think they’re going to go, I think Caleb will be paired with Kenzie, Jen with Corbin and Sol with Sincere. But I don’t think that they’re going to want to get rid of Melanie so soon, because that would leave kind of an empty drama gap. I feel like they’ll bring someone in for her soon, because she’s really bringing it when it comes to creating that conflict between her and Sincere.”

Sally Darr: “The question on the ‘Love Island’ app tonight is: ‘Who do you want to pair these bombshells up with?’ We have Sol, Caleb and Jen. I think the public will pair Sol with Sincere, Caleb with Kenzie, Jen with Gabriel. Potentially Jen with Zach, but I think they’ll do Gabriel. Based off of that, the people who are at risk are the current partners of those people: Melanie, Corbin and Bea. Those are the three Islanders at risk. I don’t know what the producers are going to do with this. My guess is they’re going to make the current islanders vote between these three people who they want to go home. I don’t know what the islanders are going to do. Are they going to send home Melanie, Bea or Corbin? There’s kind of two schools of thought with this. Do you keep the people who are building connections or do you keep the people who need more time? In previous years, specifically in the last season, I feel like the islanders were always keeping people who ‘needed more time’ or who just got here. That makes me think that they’re going to keep Corbin. I personally think you keep the people who have made connections. Honestly, none of them have a really strong connection. Currently, it’s kind of a toss-up. Corbin just got there. Bea does have a thing with Gabriel, but Gabriel’s kind of had his head turned a little bit. He’s kissing everyone. Melanie likes Sincere, but she’s kind of been moving in a weird way. I think Melanie’s kind of divisive in the villa, so maybe there’s some people that aren’t going to opt to keep her. Who’s to say? It’s going to be a toss-up if that’s what the producers do. But maybe the producers won’t make them pick, so we’ll see.”

title: How to Watch the 2026 FIFA World Cup Opening Ceremony in Mexico
summary: The 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off on Thursday, June 11, with a star-studded opening ceremony. Here is when and what time the show and match start.
story: Soccer fans around the globe are gearing up for the kick-off of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

The international men’s soccer championship features 48 teams from around the world and takes place from June 11 to July 19. For the first time ever, the tournament will be hosted by three nations: the United States, Mexico and Canada. And first up, players will face off in Los Angeles, Mexico City and Toronto.
Opening ceremonies will take place in all three locations across June 11 and June 12 with the first opening ceremony held inside Mexico City Stadium.
Mexico City Stadium will become the first venue in history to host matches across three World Cups. The stadium previously hosted World Cup matches in 1970 and 1986.

Shakira is returning to the World Cup stage, along with Colombian singer J Balvin and Mexican pop-rock band Maná, who will all perform during the opening ceremony along with fellow Latin music stars Belinda, Alejandro Fernández, Los Ángeles Azules, Lila Downs and Danny Ocean.
South African rapper Tyla will also take the stage to represent the visiting South African team, while Burna Boy is also set to perform.
The ceremony will also incorporate festive representations of the World Cup Trophy made in papel picado, a traditional Mexican craft featuring intricate designs cut into colorful tissue paper.
Read on to find out how to watch the 2026 World Cup opening ceremony.
When Does the 2026 World Cup Opening Ceremony Start?
The 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off on June 11 with an opening ceremony at Mexico City Stadium in Mexico City, Mexico.
The opening ceremony starts at 11:30 a.m. CT/10:30 a.m. PT/1:30 p.m. ET, 90 minutes before the Mexican national soccer team faces off against the team from South Africa.
How Can I Watch the 2026 World Cup Opening Ceremony?
Fans in the U.S. can watch the 2026 FIFA World Cup opening ceremony in Mexico City live on FOX and in Spanish on Telemundo.
People can also stream the event live on Peacock, FOX Sports, YouTube TV and Fubo.

title: Carley Fortune Shares the Parts of Her That Inspired the 2 ‘Every Year After’ Leads
summary: Carley Fortune speaks about the adaptation of her debut novel Every Summer After into the book Every Year After.
story: Carley Fortune is still her 10 year old self. And 13. And 16.

It’s an epiphany that came about years ago, as she re-read her journals from growing up in Barry’s Bay in Canada. A lake town — population, 1,084 — Fortune grew up down a dirt road in the woods. Her summers were spent in the waters of Kamaniskeg Lake, reading books on her dock and working at her parents’ restaurant.
On her street, three of the houses, like Fortune’s, were lived in year-round. The rest were used by cottagers, people who visited the lake with the seasons.

The main character of Fortune’s debut novel, “Every Summer After,” is one of those cottagers. Persephone Fraser spends her summers in Barry’s Bay and the rest of the year in Toronto. At 13 years old, she meets Sam Florek, who lives full time at the lake.

“Sam has exactly how I grew up,” Fortune tells TODAY.com in an interview. “He grows up on the lake in Barry’s Bay. His parents have a restaurant. He is very ambitious, and he really wants to build a life outside of his small town.”
But it’s Percy who’s “infused with so much of my insecurity and anxiety,” she says.

“Every Summer After” published in May 2022. Now it’s headed to the small screen, with an eight-episode series adaptation, retitled “Every Year After,” which dropped on Prime Video June 10. As the story comes to life before Fortune’s eyes, she’s thinking of the girl on the lake who was so desperately “longing for someone to really understand” her.

title: ‘Love Island’ Fans Can Experience the Villa at 30 Rock for 3 Days this June
summary: The Love Island villa is coming to Rockefeller Plaza from June 22 to June 24. Here’s how to find out.
story: Attention “Love Island USA” fans, the villa is heading to the states.

If you’ve ever wanted to know what it is like to get ready in the glam room for a night filled with drama and dancing or to deliver a recoupling speech at the fire pit, now is your chance. Peacock will bring iconic parts of the villa to New York City for a special three-day immersive fan event.

The pop-up will be held at Rockefeller Center and allow fans to experience the day-to-day life of an islander. Fans who visit the activation, which will be open from Monday, June 22 to Wednesday, June 24, will receive themed giveaways. They will also be able to snap pictures, create their own must-see bombshell moment and enjoy more interactive experiences.

The activation space will include build outs of the girls’ glam room, fire pit and more nods to locations around the villa. In the glam room, fans can stop by Maybelline’s shade match station and snag some beauty must-haves. At the fire pit set up, there will be a claw machine filled with CeraVe products. The skincare company will be giving out Blemish Barrier patches to fans, as well.
Register for The Villa at Rockefeller Center!
Sign up here.
Sign up here.
Additionally, fans can grab a customized bag charm that reads “Bombshell” or “Coupled Up” to let everyone know their status.
The pop-up, which is a partnership with CeraVe and Maybelline New York, will be open from 12 p.m. to 7 p.m. ET daily.

The event comes as fans are already invested in the “Love Island USA” Season 8 couples after just one week. So far, viewers have watched multiple bombshells make their grand entrances and one original islander get dumped during the first recoupling of the season. Fans can continue to follow along with the islanders as new episodes of the reality dating show arrive every day, except Wednesdays, at 9 p.m. ET / 6 p.m. PT on Peacock.

title: Jenna and Sheinelle Answer 2 Burning Questions from Fans, And They’re Looking for More
summary: Jenna Bush Hager and Sheinelle Jones are giving viewers a look at the inner workings of their show and friendship in a new segment called “Ask Away.”The TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle co-hosts debuted the segment June 9 and shared two questions from fans of the show, which were submitted on Instagram.
story: Jenna Bush Hager and Sheinelle Jones are giving viewers a look at the inner workings of their show and friendship in a new segment called “Ask Away.”
The TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle co-hosts debuted the segment June 9 and shared two questions from fans of the show, which were submitted on Instagram.
Up first, they answered the following query: “Do you have a favorite memory together?”
Sheinelle went first and recalled a time when the TODAY crew hung out on a rooftop in New York City for the whole day.
“About how many years ago was that?” she asked Jenna.
“Three or four or five,” Jenna replied.
“It started around, say, noon. Would you like to tell America when it ended?” Sheinelle said.
“Nine p.m., right?” Jenna asked.
Sheinelle continued, trying to remember if Dylan Dreyer had “chipped her tooth” at the get together. Then, she realized she’d actually “skinned her knee.”
“We haven’t done a fun lunch in a long time. But we like to do that. It’s really fun,” Sheinelle said.
Next, it was Jenna’s turn to share her favorite memory with her co-host, which occurred during one of many Olympics games TODAY has covered over the years.
“We were kind of on standby, so we didn’t do much. And we spent the entire time, or I did, learning TikTok dances with Sheinelle,” she said.
“It was a good time,” Sheinelle agreed.
“It was fun. It’s what the kids do, bro,” Jenna joked.
For their second and final question of the day, the co-hosts offered insight into their on-air wardrobe while answering the question, “Do you choose your own clothes?”
While the co-hosts often do pick out their own outfits, Sheinelle said she got a bit of help this morning.
“I was going to do something Knicks-related, and then they didn’t win, so I was like, ‘OK, well, let me keep the top,’” she said of her orange blouse.
Sheinelle then explained that one of her colleagues suggested she wear a pair of brown pants.
“I think it looks chic,” Jenna said.
“I’ve never worn orange and brown,” Sheinelle said.
Jenna, who was wearing a khaki dress, then suggested that she and Sheinelle “both kind of look like we’re going on some sort of safari.”
Keep an eye on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle’s Instagram for another chance to submit questions.

title: ‘Glass Half Full’: Rich Paul Opens up About Moving from Friends to More with Adele
summary: Rich Paul tells Craig Melvin’s “Glass Half Full” how he met Adele and they wound up going from friends to a couple.
story: Rich Paul represents some of the biggest names in sports, but it’s how he landed a relationship with one of the biggest names in music that he’s talking about now.
The Klutch Sports Group founder, agent and entrepreneur opened up about meeting longtime partner Adele on the June 9 episode of Craig Melvin’s “Glass Half Full” podcast.
“How did you meet Adele?” Craig asked.

“Through a friend. I had known her for some time,” Paul said. “You know, just when you’re in these circles, man, you’re in these circles. But I never tried to get fresh with people that’s in comfortable circles because they always have to deal with that, so that was never my thing. It was really something that happened very organically, really.”
Paul, who represents stars like LeBron James, Anthony Davis, A’ja Wilson and Tyrese Maxey, said he wasn’t too familiar with her music before they met.

“Obviously, you can’t help but to hear the monster hits,” he said when asked if he had previously listed to Adele’s music. “But, no, not exactly. But obviously, you know, I’m pretty on the pulse of a lot of different things, and so, you know, some of the songs, the big songs, but I didn’t know all of the joints that I know now.”
Pressed for more details on the origins of their relationship, Paul said they continued to run into each other.
“We’re all in the same circles, and, you know, we’re hanging or whatnot,” he said. “We just used to always see each other, laugh and joke. It was just cordial, really, just cordial.”
“Until?” Craig said.
“Until it became not so cordial. Until I became a person of interest,” he joked, prompting Craig to laugh.
“A suspect,” quipped Craig.
Paul also may be with one of the world’s biggest pop stars, but he said he doesn’t shy away from showing off his pipes.
“I’m the best singer in the house,” he joked.

Adele and Paul started dating in 2021 and have kept their relationship relatively private. The two sparked engagement rumors back in 2024 after Adele responded to a fan’s poster at her concert with, “‘Will you marry me?’ I can’t marry you because I’m already getting married, so I can’t.”
Watch Craig’s full conversation with Paul for “Glass Half Full” on TODAY’s YouTube channel or listen wherever you get your podcasts.

title: How ‘Off Campus’ Actor Charlie Evans Kept the Biggest Secret of Season 1
summary: “Off Campus” actor Charlie Evans, who plays Hunter Davenport, tells TODAY.com about keeping his character’s identity a secret and what’s to come in Season 2.
story: After the cliffhanger finale of “Off Campus” Season 1, questions united longtime fans of the books, new viewers and several of the show’s characters: Who is Hunter Davenport? And what’s he doing here?

Played by “Paradise” actor Charlie Evans, Hunter is initially introduced in Episode 7 as a stranger in a bar, Carter St. James, though this turns out to be an alias for his fake ID. He and Allie Hayes (Mika Abdalla) share a kiss as she tries to prove she can embrace a single, unattached life amid her post-breakup fling with Dean Di Laurentis (Stephen Kalyn).

“Carter” crops up again in Episode 8 running shirtless past Allie after she and Dean agree to an “assignment” to prove the casual nature of their relationship. She completes that “assignment” (sleeping with someone else) with “Carter.”

But in the final moments of Season 1, we learn — in rapid succession — that Dean did not sleep with someone else; Dean and Hunter Davenport have a longstanding, intense feud; and “Carter St. James” is Hunter Davenport.

“I signed on for Carter St. James, so even in the audition process, I didn’t know who he was, really,” Evans tells TODAY.com. “Then when I found out, they’re like, ‘But we’re going to do this whole thing, it’s going to be revealed, so we’ve got to really stay on top of keeping it a secret.’
“That was the whole year for me, just keeping my lips sealed,” he adds. “Anytime anyone asked, ‘Why are you in Canada?’ ... I was like, ‘Oh, just shooting a thing.’”
When “Off Campus” author Elle Kennedy heard about the Hunter Davenport twist, she had some reservations.

“I was a little worried, because for new people who have not read the books, I was like, they’re going to be like, ‘Who’s Hunter Davenport?’” Kennedy tells TODAY.com. “But for people who have read the books it was like ... jaw-dropping.”

Hunter is a Briar University hockey player who’s younger than the core group at the center of “Off Campus.” While he appears in several books of the first series, he doesn’t step into the spotlight until Kennedy’s spinoff series, “Briar U,” specifically Book 3, “The Play.”

But he’s shaping up to play a big role in Season 2, with questions still lingering: What’s his potential connection to Dean’s sister? What will his and Allie’s hookup mean for her and Dean? And how will he fit into Briar’s starting hockey lineup?
Below, Evans opens up about the casting process, keeping his character’s identity a secret and what’s ahead for the sophomore season.
This interview has been lightly edited for clarity.

Once I’d been cast, we kind of had to let everyone know that the plan was to keep me a secret. So no following anyone on social media, no pictures together, no posting that I was in Vancouver, or that I was near the set. Stuff like that, because people are quite diligent in their research, and they can piece together things really quickly. These fans are quite smart. Frighteningly so.

We had to cover all our bases in that way, and everyone was on board, and everyone was super supportive. I didn’t feel left out, which was very helpful. Even though I was hiding, it was still quite collaborative, which was nice.

I did a lot of research on the character. I’m currently reading the book. I’m not done yet, so I can’t speak too much about that, but I tried to glean as much as I could from (showrunner) Louisa (Levy) and from online, and just sort of collect as much as I could on the character before going in. But not completely done with the book yet.

I like it. I like Hunter. I think it’s cool that we get to see a different side of Hunter. At least in the show, we get a couple moments where he’s more vulnerable, or, I guess — that scene with Mika (Abdalla, who plays Allie) has some signs that he’s not as staunch as he lets on, but obviously that’s explored a lot more in the books. So I’m very excited to read more about that.

I was aware that Hunter has his own story. And I think because the way Louisa set up the show is more like a network of these stories. Kind of setting up a continuity in the universe, where these people just exist, and they live, and they can come in at random times and meet people that they may not have expected to meet. It makes it feel alive and moving and natural, you know, like a real college campus. It’s not as organized, the people aren’t as separated, and there is crossover. And I think it was really brilliant to feed that in at the end and kind of shed light on this larger world that exists within “Off Campus.”

Oh, it was beautiful. It was so, so collaborative because, you know, we’re a bunch of kids, pretty much. Everyone gets along so well, but there’s also a real sense of, like, we’re doing something really special that we all care about. So everyone’s so invested every day on set. There’s no weak links in this cast, and there’s no, you know, potholes for us to fall through. Everyone’s on the same track. We all want the same thing for the show, we all want the same thing for each other, and there’s support for that every day on set. And it’s such a family, it really is.

I knew it was going to be popular just based on Louisa’s writing and the cast chemistry. I mean, it was kind of a perfect storm in terms of just seeing movies get made. Sometimes you see red flags or green flags of how they’re going to come out, and I always knew it was going to be good. But I’m really astonished by the support for the show, and how much people are enjoying it.

And, you know, I go to my other work to shoot my other show, and people are talking about it and asking questions, and I’m really, really grateful that people are enjoying it as much as they have. It’s really nice.
He’s a sophomore, I believe. So he’s younger than everyone else, that was very clear.

On campus, I’d say he’s — I think Hunter wants to appear maybe more of a ladies man than he is. I think his true passion and his real time commitment is hockey. I think it is the sport, and I think he is quite diligent because he has a lot of history and trauma surrounding it, and so I feel like he has this — kind of how Garrett had, he was very wrapped up in it in more ways than just, we’re playing in college, we’re having fun.

I think Hunter has a lot to prove in that realm, and I think that’s what makes him and Dean’s relationship so much more interesting than just the social aspects, because there is this competition, on and off the ice. (They) talked about it in that final scene, I think, like, “You’re scared I’m going to dust your a-- again?” You know, stuff like that. He has a lot to prove, and I can’t wait to prove it. (Laughs.) I guess.

I’ve been on the ice before. It’s not something I’m completely new to. I’m not any Stephen Kalyn. But yeah, I went and I did the two-week hockey camp with the boys in Vancouver, which was a lot of fun and transformative for my hockey skills — which isn’t saying a lot, but it is to me. And I’m not falling over anymore, which is nice.

We did it a few months ago. This was in preparation for Season 2. I think there’s going to be another one, but we’re really just cracking down on the hockey, want to make sure everyone’s on point. Because everyone wants to do their own stuff. We have, obviously, stuntmen but it’s surprising how much us actors are actually doing that stuff on the ice, and want to for Season 2.
We’ll see how it goes, but everyone’s gotten exceptionally good. I mean, like, really, really exceptionally good. They’ve leveled up incredibly. It’s really cool to see.

Outside of the characters, whether they like each other or not, we all get along very well on the ice. I mean, it’s just a bunch of boys who have nothing better to do on a 7 a.m. morning for two weeks, just running into walls and each other, and learning. We just had the most gracious, supportive, kind coaches and support from “Off Campus” production team, in terms of just the help that we needed to get where we needed to be on the ice.
I would say that Stephen’s there, Antonio’s there, Jalen’s there, Belmont’s there — and I’m getting there.

The vulnerability. I’m excited to see a different side of Hunter and break down those walls, and maybe see reasons why he has become the way he is. Why he’s so protective of himself and of his image, and hopefully explore an arc where he kind of breaks that down a bit, and you see a more real, more vulnerable side of Hunter. I’m hoping for that.

title: Andy Cohen Confirms Dating Rumors: ‘I Met the Person’
summary: Andy Cohen has confirmed those relationship rumors. Here’s what he had to say about the new relationship including how they met.
story: Andy Cohen has confirmed those relationship rumors. He is, indeed, taken — and in love.
The host of Bravo’s “Watch What Happens Live” opened up on his SiriusXM podcast, “Andy Cohen Live,” about how he has “met the person.”
“This man is kind, and strong, and smart, and he is an adult,” Cohen said on his podcast. “And he loves my children, and they love him, and I love him.”
Chatter online first began June 4 when Deux Moi, the popular celebrity gossip social media account, posted a paparazzi photo across its platforms.

Appearing on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle June 4, pop culture expert Nessa Diab shared some details of this new summer romance, including the rumored identity behind the mystery “businessman”: Kevin Sobiesky.
“You know it’s serious, at least serious enough, to be, you know, claiming each other,” Diab said. “Fingers crossed.”
“We’re happy for him,” Jenna and Sheinelle replied in near unison.
While Cohen didn’t confirm his new boyfriend’s name during “Andy Cohen Live,” he did disclose other details about their romance, including when they met.
According to Cohen, they crossed paths at a mutual friend’s July 4 party last year.
Cohen said he dragged himself to because he never gets invited to “gay parties at the Hamptons anymore.” Then, he met someone with whom he immediately connected.

“We talked all night: I very methodically put my number in his phone,” Cohen said. “Apparently, I said to him twice that night, ‘I want to make sure this number is right … and then he said, ‘Wow, I knew you were serious when you were really, like, serious about this number.’”
Cohen also shared how he introduced his new boyfriend to his children, Benjamin Allen and Lucy Eve.

“We have been so slow and deliberate and methodical about getting to know each other, and introducing him to the kids, and just entering him into the equation of my family so slowly,” Cohen said. “It has been so easy. One year later, I still can’t believe I found him.”
Cohen has a message for audiences itching to know more about the new couple.
“Thank you for giving me space here, not that you had a choice,” Cohen said. “I just wanted to be smart and slow before saying anything about this.”

title: An ‘Off Campus’ Star’s Surprise Concert Appearance Is a Major Nod to the Show’s Fans
summary: Why is Belmont Cameli’s dance with Kid Laroi going viral? Explaining the viral moment and why the Garrett Graham dance is something poeple love to hate.
story: Four weeks after “Off Campus” premiered and swept the zeitgeist, actor Belmont Cameli is still trending –– this time, for sparking his own TikTok dance trend.

Cameli portrays Garrett Graham in the Amazon Prime Video series “Off Campus,” which premiered May 13. The series is an adaptation of Elle Kennedy’s bestselling novel series of the same name. The series follows the drama-filled ecosystem surrounding a college men’s hockey team at the fictional Briar University.

In Episode 1, to get the attention of love interest Hannah Wells (Ella Bright), Cameli’s character dances to The Kid LAROI’s song “Girls.” At first, Garrett is reluctant to make any moves, hunching over and laughing at all the attention. Garrett eventually lets loose and gets into what is now his signature move, which consists of just a shy hip shake and some low fist pumps back and forth.
Cameli’s dancing abilities contrasts with Garrett’s discomfort on the dance floor. In an interview with Her Campus, Bright and Cameli said filming the now viral scene was tough as a result.

“Can I just say, Belmont is an insane dancer, and he was always Amastruggling with the fact that Garrett doesn’t dance, and he was like, ‘I can’t show, like, my true ability in this scene,’” Bright said.
“I can really cut a rug,” Cameli added. “They were like, ‘Garrett doesn’t dance, Garrett doesn’t dance.”

After this scene went viral, videos surfaced across social media of the actor dancing in all kinds of places — at a party, on set with co-stars, or just randomly on the street. One fan coined the phenomenon as “Belmont dancing core.”

Cameli even took his moves directly to The Kid LAROI himself. At the singer’s concert at the Greek Theatre in Los Angeles June 6, the actor brought some “Off Campus” vibes to the crowd with a re-creation of Garrett’s moves. Fans have loved it, posting multiple edits and angles.
The Kid LAROI HQ’s video on TikTok earned nearly 10 million views. The Kid LAROI and Cameli teamed up for a dance collab in Garrett’s honor, too.

Despite the dance routine not being very elaborate or long, fans have rushed to TikTok to re-create it. Co-stars Cameli and Bright stopped by “The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon” May 23, where they gave Fallon a dance tutorial.

One fan taught the viral dance to her boyfriend. Are the videos poking fun at the quality of the dance? Are they truly out of love for the character? Either way, Garrett’s scene from Episode 1 has sparked a chain of dance breaks across the internet.
Kennedy spoke with TODAY.com about the future of the book series amid the viral success of the television adaptation.

“Seeing the response to the show and how people are resonating with Hannah and Garrett, and it’s this relationship that I came up with a decade ago, seeing it on screen and seeing it play out has just been so cool,” Kennedy told TODAY.com.

Confirmation of a Season 2 of “Off Campus” rolled in quickly. The series was renewed by Prime Video in February, nearly four months prior to the premiere in May. The next season will focus on a different couple in this Briar University social circle.

title: Rob Mac Recalls Watching One of Soccer’s ‘Greatest Legends’ Play in the World Cup as a Child
summary: Rob McElhenney, also known as Rob Mac, talks about his earliest World Cup memories and partnering with Enterprise.
story: Rob McElhenney’s knowledge of the World Cup and soccer — or should we say, football — has come a long way.

The actor, also known as Rob Mac, describes the global competition as “the biggest tournament in the world,” in an interview with TODAY.com. “And please put an asterisk that I called it football throughout the interview, but I will call it soccer here,” he adds.

Mac, 49, “distinctly” remembers being a young kid and finding out a friend had posters of one specific soccer star, Diego Maradona. Watching the Argentine legend on television changed the way the Emmy winner saw the sport and, coincidently, his life.

These days, the “It’s Always Sunny in Philadelphia” star’s ties to soccer include being the co-owner of the Welsh professional football club Wrexham AFC with Ryan Reynolds, and partnering with Enterprise during the 2026 World Cup.

The car company is giving away a car for every World Cup corner kick goal, starting June 11 when the tournament officially begins. Just like being a part of the biggest soccer tournament in the world is impactful, Mac says a new car “can change someone’s life.”

With over 1,000 chances for fans to win, Mac says he has “such a vested interest now in every single game.” He is also excited to see different countries and players come together for the worldwide event.
Below, Mac shares his World Cup memories, as well as what he’s most looking forward to as the tournament kicks off.

I was a little kid and I remember being over at a friend’s house, Tommy Reynolds, and that was a big soccer household. And they said, “You have to see this guy,” and he had posters all over his wall. Everybody else had Michael Jordan posters. I had Randall Cunningham posters, who played in the NFL. People had Magic Johnson and Larry Bird. He had Diego Maradona posters everywhere, and I didn’t know who Diego Maradona was.
The very first time I watched the World Cup was watching Diego Maradona play.

I was a little kid and I remember distinctly watching it with (my friend) and his family and people going nuts! And I thought, “Huh, I don’t know, this seems like a fun sport.” I didn’t realize I was watching one of the greatest legends in the history of the game.
I would say my biggest supporter for the last 20 years has probably been my wife, (Kaitlin Olson).

Additionally, (Chairman of FX Networks) John Landgraf has been a huge champion and supporter of mine from the very beginning. I was in the service industry when we created “Sunny” and he entrusted me to run the television series and he’s been supporting me ever since. Even when I brought him the crazy idea of buying a football team, he bought a documentary around that and he understood.

I’d say my biggest competition has been my own insecurities, anxiety and fear, and it’s just something that I have to navigate and push through. Not pretend like it’s not there, but process it, synthesize it, try to exercise it and move on.

We’ve got so much external competition, sure. On the pitch, off the pitch, you have people who drive you competitively. But at the end of the day, no matter what, they don’t hold that much power over you. They are igniting something inside of you, they’re triggering something, whether it’s a feeling of fear or consternation, or even excitement, or anger, or rage. That’s not something that they’re really eliciting. It’s a stimulus that they’re creating. And then you’re either allowing it to take over, or you’re just observing it, processing it and moving forward.

I’m looking forward to every single corner kick, so now I’m doubly invested because we have a few players from Wrexham that are actually playing. One for Scotland and one for New Zealand, so I’m going to be biased towards them.

I have my U.S. heritage, obviously. So U.S. I’ll be rooting for No. 1. I live in Los Angeles, and I have so many Latino friends and Mexican friends who are die-hard Mexican fans, so that’s going to be really fun. And now also watching every single match, every corner kick is an opportunity for somebody to win a car. So I’m going to be watching every single one of these games, excited for somebody.

There’s just so much pride. And to be able to represent your home country, I see it happen with our players, our international players. It is such an honor and a joy for them to stop everything they’re doing and go back home and they’re able to play for their teams. Wearing that badge, I can see it ignites something from their childhood. It’s a great honor for them.

title: Rosie O’Donnell Makes First Public Appearance Since Sharing She Had a Facelift
summary: Rosie O’Donnell stepped out at the Tony Awards in her first public appearance since revealing that she had a facelift early this year.
story: Rosie O’Donnell stepped out at the Tony Awards in her first public appearance since revealing that she had a facelift early this year.

The comedian and former co-host of “The View” took in the annual awards ceremony in New York City on June 7 celebrating the best of Broadway, where she spoke about her decision to reveal in a  Substack essay on May 25 that she had a lower deep plane facelift in January.

“I think all that matters is truth and love,” she told E! News on the Tonys red carpet. “And so I wanted to be truthful and say all the complicated emotions I had about it. But I just felt it was better to be truthful than not, and I didn’t want some tabloid to go, ‘Gotcha!’

“I just wanted to say this is what I did, and here’s the doctor, and if you want to,” she continued. “But it’s very expensive. It’s more expensive than any car I ever bought, but I can’t drive around in my face.”
O’Donnell, 64, who lives in Ireland with her 13-year-old son, showed off her new look on the red carpet while dressed in a black suit. She also had praise for Pink, who hosted the Tony Awards.

“PINK SLAYED THE CROWD AT THE TONYS — magical brilliance- a true rock god — belting with the best of them — dancing fosse with ease — she is everything ❤️❤️❤️❤️-congratulations #pink #tonys #perfect” she wrote.
Fans complimented her red carpet look.
“Rosie, you look incredible ❤️” one wrote on Instagram.
“You look great, Rosie! Good to see you here again. 💖✨️” another commented.
“Looking great, Rosie! 👍🏼💙” another wrote.
The actor and comedian also shared on Instagram that her one-woman show, “Common Knowledge,” is coming to New York City in July and August after a tour in Europe.

In her Substack essay, O’Donnell wrote that she underwent a procedure that lifts tissue under the facial muscles to avoid the skin looking too tight. She also shared before-and-after photos on Instagram.
The “A League of Their Own” star wrote about her struggles in deciding to have the facelift.

“I used to feel very strongly about facelifts. Not casually — morally. I had assigned myself as head of all women who would never — ever,” she wrote. “I thought it was a betrayal. Of feminism. Of aging. Of our team of women worldwide. And then I lost 50 pounds…”
O’Donnell said her face had gotten a “haunted” look after her dramatic weight loss.
She wrote that the procedure made her look like “a slightly more well-rested emotionally stable version of me.”
“I didn’t disappear, I didn’t become someone else — I just stopped arguing with the mirror. And maybe that’s enough,” she added.

title: Wendy’s Drops New ‘Minions’ Frosty — and You Can Probably Guess the Flavor
summary: Ahead of the release of “Minions & Monsters,” Wendy’s is dropping a Minions-themed kids meal, adult meal and Banana Swirl Frosty. The TODAY anchors try them.
story: Minions mania is coming to a Wendy’s near you this summer.
To celebrate the upcoming release of “Minions & Monsters,” the latest addition to the “Despicable Me” franchise, Wendy’s is debuting a few limited-edition menu items.
Minions fans will have to wait until July 1 to see the new movie, but Wendy’s is kicking off the fun a bit early.
Here’s everything you need to know about the Minions-inspired additions to the Wendy’s menu and the TODAY anchors’ reactions to one of the new items.
Available Now: Wendy’s ‘Minions & Monsters’ Kids Meal
If your little one can hardly wait for “Minions & Monsters” to hit theaters, you can hold them over in the meantime with a Wendy’s kids meal inspired by the film.
Each meal comes with the following items:

Your choice of two-piece chicken tenders, four-piece chicken nuggets, a hamburger or cheeseburger Jr. Hot & Crispy Fries or apple bites Kids drinkOne of the following six “Minions & Monsters” toys: James, Henry, Ed, Richard, Goomi or Dort
Your choice of two-piece chicken tenders, four-piece chicken nuggets, a hamburger or cheeseburger
Jr. Hot & Crispy Fries or apple bites
Kids drink
One of the following six “Minions & Monsters” toys: James, Henry, Ed, Richard, Goomi or Dort
Available June 15: ‘Minions & Monsters’-Inspired Frosty, Meal & Beverages
On June 15, Wendy’s is releasing a “Minions & Monsters” adult meal, a Banana Frosty Swirl and two new Coca-Cola Freestyle beverages.
The Banana Frosty Swirl is made with a Vanilla Frosty base and a sweet banana cream sauce that lends the dessert a neon yellow vibe.
Adults are big fans of the “Despicable Me” franchise, too! The “Minions & Monsters” adult meal comes with the following items:
Your choice of a Big Bacon Classic or the new Spicy Chicken SandwichSmall Hot & Crispy Fries Small Banana Frosty SwirlOne of four “Minions & Monsters” blind box collectibles
Your choice of a Big Bacon Classic or the new Spicy Chicken Sandwich
Small Hot & Crispy Fries
Small Banana Frosty Swirl
One of four “Minions & Monsters” blind box collectibles
Wendy’s is also releasing two new drinks inspired by the film:
Pineapple Minion MischieFizz Goomi’s Glimey Lime
Pineapple Minion MischieFizz
Goomi’s Glimey Lime
The TODAY Anchors React to the New Banana Frosty Swirl
After exclusively announcing the new Banana Frosty Swirl on air on June 8, several members of the TODAY family put the new sweet treat to the test.
Jenna Bush Hager’s initial reaction was short and sweet as she said, “It’s not bad.”
“Not at all,” Craig Melvin agreed.
“The chocolate one’s pretty good,” Al Roker added.
Savannah Guthrie was hesitant at first and said, “I’m trying to avoid the banana part of it.”
She went on to explain that the bright-yellow hue “disturbed her.”
“Have you seen a Minion?” Al teased.
“Well, I know, but I don’t want to eat a Minion,” Savannah replied.
Jenna admitted she was a bit “nervous” at first to try the treat, but “didn’t need to be.”
In the end, Savannah acknowledged that the drink didn’t have an overwhelming banana taste.
“Actually, this tastes good. I like the chocolate one,” she said.

title: Pink’s Performance As Tonys Host Gets Rave Reviews. She Was Just Trying to Impress Her 15-Year-Old
summary: Pink pulled off a star-studded Tony Awards opening performance that earned her a standing ovation
story: Pink pulled off a star-studded Tony Awards opening performance that earned her a standing ovation.
The singer was joined on stage by numerous dancers, singers and celebrities who made cameos during a lively performance of “Lady Marmalade.”

It’s a familiar song for Pink, who previously recorded a version of the tune alongside other singers for the 2001 film “Moulin Rouge!” This time, she playfully changed the lyrics and gave shoutouts to a plethora of Broadway stars during the performance.

Rapper Megan Thee Stallion, who made her Broadway debut this year in “Moulin Rouge! The Musical,” made an appearance toward the end of the song. Several Broadway stars also supported Pink throughout the performance, including Lea Michele, Marla Mindelle and more.
At the end of the performance, audience members gave Pink a standing ovation. Viewers were also impressed with the fanfare and took to social media to give the performance their seal of approval.
Instagram users called the performance “outstanding,”  “iconic,” “spectacular” and “amazing.”
“Pink performs like no other!” one commented on a video of the opening performance.
Another shared the following reaction: “She outsang all of broadway!”
Before she launched into her performance, Pink did a short skit with Neil Patrick Harris and showed off her acrobatic skills as she poked fun at herself for being selected to host the awards.
“I just want to show how much I love theater even though I’ve never been on Broadway,” she said.
“I’m just concerned people might be like, ‘Why’s Pink hosting the Tonys?’” she added.

To celebrate her first time hosting the Tony Awards, Pink made the night a family affair and appeared on the blue carpet with her husband, Carey Hart, 15-year-old daughter Willow, 9-year-old son  Jameson and her mother, Judith Moore.
During her opening monologue, Pink gave a shoutout to her daughter.
“Tonight, I am here as Broadway’s biggest fan — well second biggest, next to my daughter, Willow,” she said.
“I don’t know where she is, but I did not take this job so my kid could get Broadway selfies, although (we did get her a few) … and a couple for mama,” she continued.
When she first announced that she was hosting the awards show, Pink revealed that she first got permission from her daughter to take the job.

“When I asked my daughter, she was really excited about being able to have a ticket to go to the Tonys,” she said at the time. “So I’m hosting the Tonys, and I’m really, really excited and very nervous because that girl is a tough crowd!”

title: Mindy Kaling Shares How ‘Not Suitable for Work’ Wraps Up a Trilogy of Shows Loosely Based on Her Life
summary: Mindy Kaling appears on Sunday Sitdown with Willie Geist to chat about new series “Not Suitable for Work” and how it ends a chapter of her career.
story: Mindy Kaling is back with a new series that concludes her trilogy of shows inspired by the early days of her career.

The actor, writer, director and producer recently released “Not Suitable for Work,” a comedy about five twenty-somethings who are pursuing their career aspirations in Manhattan. The first three episodes of the series arrived on Hulu June 2. Kaling, who created the show and also serves as executive producer, chatted with Willie Geist on Sunday Sitdown about why “Not Suitable for Work” marks the end of an era.

“I think of ‘Not Suitable for Work’ as the last show in this trilogy of shows that I’ve done about young people,” Kaling explained. “‘Never Have I Ever’ was a show loosely based on my childhood and the loss of a parent. ‘Sex Lives with College Girls’ was very loosely based on my time at Dartmouth.”
She laughed and clarified that that series was less autobiographical. “Not that much sex happening there,” she joked.

“Never Have I Ever,” which was a coming-of-age story focused on high school students, ran for four seasons on Netflix and ended in June 2023. “Sex Lives with College Girls,” an HBO Max show about a group of college freshmen, concluded in March 2025 after three seasons.

Kaling shared that “Not Suitable for Work” is based on her experience in her early 20s when she relocated to New York City. She described the period as an “ambitious” chapter. “I just wanted to hurry up to the time in my life when I was successful,” she recalled.
The 46-year-old said that she didn’t intend for the three series to serve as a trilogy of her life story.
“That’s just the pretentious way that I like to frame my career now,” she quipped.
When Willie asked if Kaling romanticizes her 20s, she referenced a quote from “The Office,” the show that gave her a breakout role and her first major job as a staff writer, too.

“There’s this line in ‘The Office’ that I love, from one of the later seasons,” she said, quoting Ed Helms’ character in the final season when he says, “I wish there was a way to know that you’re in the good old days before you’ve actually left them.”

“I think that’s such a profound thought,” she said. “Because that time when I was in New York, and I was surrounded by friends, and none of us had money, it was the good old days. But all I could see is, like, what I didn’t have. It makes me want to be more grateful for what I have now.”

In addition to her work in television, Kaling also debuted publishing imprint Mindy’s Book Studio, a partnership with Amazon, in 2022. The books she chooses for her label share similarities with the types of shows she has created, which include series like “The Mindy Project” and “Running Point.”

“I love juicy stories, page turners for TV shows. Ones with big characters (and) big plot devices… But I just happen to populate the world with people that you don’t normally see in TV. And that’s kind of my philosophy behind the books that I pick,” she shared.
Willie noted that Kaling is now in a stage of her career where she can help actors and authors who might usually be overlooked in Hollywood get a chance to showcase their talent.
“It’s the best part of my job,” she agreed. Although she acknowledges that wasn’t always her goal.

“When I started out, I wasn’t thinking about pulling other people up. I was like, ‘I just want health insurance and to make enough money that I can prove to my parents it wasn’t stupid to move to L.A,” she explained.
But over the years, she has recognized her importance in the industry.

“Then after a while I realized, ‘Oh, when you’re the only person who does something, it means more than your sort of selfish interests for clout and fame, and everything else,’” she added. “It means something to other people.”
It is also significant to her that she has made her parents, who came to the U.S. as immigrants, proud.
“My dad is so supportive,” she told Willie, adding how as a single mom, she’s fortunate her father is “still alive and well. “He and my stepmom (are) such a huge part of the lives of my children.”
While Kaling wishes her mom, who died of pancreatic cancer in 2012 could see her success and meet her three children, she is still grateful.
“I do have so many other blessings,” she said.

title: ‘Mexico 86’ Star Diego Luna Breaks Down the True Story That Inspired the Film
summary: Diego Luna’s new Netflix movie, “Mexico 86,” is loosely based on how Mexico obtained the World Cup 40 years ago.  Here is the true story behind the movie and if Martin de la Torre is a real person.
story: This June, the 2026 FIFA World Cup will take place in three countries, the United States, Mexico and Canada.

Forty-eight teams will give it their all for a chance to represent their countries and secure the top soccer trophy. This year marks the first year the U.S. is hosting the World Cup since 1994, but 40 years ago, Mexico fought hard to be the sole location of the tournament.

The story is seemingly depicted in “Mexico 86,” a new Spanish-language Netflix movie starring Diego Luna, which tells the dramatized story of how against all odds the country came out on top to obtain the event.

“I got involved with this film before the script was even finished,” Luna, who also serves as an executive producer, tells TODAY.com in Spanish. “I got involved because of the promise of what the project represented.”
A true underdog story and one that sees Mexico triumph, “Mexico 86” is directed by Gabriel Ripstein, with Karla Souza, Daniel Giménez Cacho, Davor Tomic and Álvaro Guerrero co-starring.
With another World Cup on its hand, Mexico is the only country to host the tournament three times. Read on to learn what went down in 1986 and what is fact and fiction in “Mexico 86.”
Is ‘Mexico 86’ Based on a True Story?
Yes, “Mexico 86” is loosely based on the events of how Mexico won the bid to host the 1986 World Cup.

The North American country first hosted in 1970, and was not set to host the tournament in 1986. It was originally set to take place in Colombia, but due to economic factors the nation had to withdraw.
FIFA (Fédération Internationale de Football Association) had to scramble to find a new host with many nations, including the U.S., Canada and Mexico, hoping to secure the event.

A 21-member executive committee made the “unanimous decision” for Mexico to host after Canada reportedly “offered too few stadiums for the tournament and that the United States Government had not provided sufficient logistical guarantees,” The New York Times reported in 1983.

U.S. Secretary of State Henry A. Kissinger had been working to secure the nation as host, but ultimately the FIFA president at the time, João Havelange, selected Mexico. According to the Times, FIFA “refused to send inspecting delegations” to the U.S. and Canada, after inspecting facilities in Mexico.
“Mexico 86” shows at one point, how vice president of FIFA Hermann Neuberger visited Mexico and the country’s bid became official.
However, it wasn’t an easy task to pull off. In 1985, Mexico City suffered a devastating earthquake that killed more than 12,000 people and had to rebuild the city, the Associated Press reported. The

As the film notes, in 1986, Mexico was in an economic crisis of its own with unemployment and the public disillusioned with politicians. But after securing the World Cup, for a month that year, Mexico “felt like the center of the universe.”
“Mexico 86” specifically shows how one determined man, Martín de la Torre, did everything in his power to get Mexico the World Cup.
Is Martín de la Torre a Real Person?
Described as a “cunning Mexican bureaucrat” in the film’s synopsis, Martín de la Torre is not a real person.
Luna shares that de la Torre is a culmination of “many executives from that time.”

“We didn’t want to commit to a specific name so we could include all the anecdotes we’d heard, the ones we know happened, fill in the blanks,” the actor says, adding they also wanted to touch on  “legendary stories, the ones that who knows if they actually happened but are still told.”
Luna says that de la Torre represents the Mexican Football Federation.

“Many things we know happened with the Federation, in this case, we attributed to the character,” he explains. “And we wanted the freedom to also tell a fictional story, because it’s not a documentary or a journalistic report, it’s not a journalistic investigation.”
He says it’s a fun “film based on real events, on things we know happened and others that we invented.”
His approach when creating de la Torre, he says, was to portray a “man full of contradictions, vices, whose actions are questionable.”
“He’s neither good nor bad, he lives in between and is trying to be the best version of himself,” Luna says. “But, his vices end up betraying him.”
The result is an enjoyable and comical tale of underdogs doing what they have to to get the job done.
What Happened at the 1986 World Cup?
In short, Mexico hosted 24 teams for the 1986 World Cup. In the final, held at the Estadio Azteca in Mexico City on June 29, were Argentina and West Germany.
Argentina took home their second World Cup title, winning 3-2. The man leading Argentina was Diego Maradona, considered one of the greatest soccer players of all time.
The 2026 World Cup kicks off in Mexico on June 11 with the star0-studded opening ceremony and match between Mexico and South Africa.

title: Sarah Michelle Gellar and ‘Buffy’ Cast Share Emotional Tributes for Late Co-Star Anthony Head
summary: Sarah Michelle Gellar, David Boreanaz, James Marsters and more “Buffy” actors honored Anthony Head’s legacy after news of his death.
story: Sarah Michelle Gellar’s tribute to her “Buffy the Vampire Slayer” co-star pulls at the heartstrings.
Anthony Head, best known as Giles and Buffy’s ever-faithful “watcher” on the supernatural series, died at the age of 72, his daughters, Emily and Daisy, confirmed on June 5.

In a fitting and emotional tribute, Gellar shared photos of her with Head over the years. Some of the snaps included fellow “Buffy” co-star Seth Green, as well as husband Freddie Prinze Jr. and a memorable moment between Giles and Buffy.
Gellar began her tribute with a quote from the Season 5 finale.

“‘Tell Giles I figured it out and I’m ok,’” Gellar began, quoting her character before she sacrifices herself to save the world. She then added, “Well I don’t have it figured out and I’m not ok. But I know I’m the lucky one because I knew you.”
“Thank you to Daisy and Emily who not only shared their dad with me, but with the world,” Gellar concluded.

The final slide in her tribute is a scene from the Season 2 episode titled “Lie to Me” when Buffy asks if life ever gets easier. When Giles asks her what she wants him to say, she tells him to lie to her.

“Yes, it’s terribly simple. The good guys are always stalwart and true. The bad guys are easily distinguished by their pointy horns or black hats. And we always defeat them and save the day. No one ever dies and everybody lives happily ever after,” he replies.
Head’s daughters announced in a statement shared with NBC News that their father “passed away peacefully of complications due to pneumonia, surrounded by his family.”

“We know how dearly he will be missed by friends, colleagues, and fans of the shows he was in — he loved his job very much, and he always considered himself incredibly lucky, to have been able to work alongside such exceptionally talented people, in such wonderful productions, across a career that spanned several decades,” they wrote in part.
A number of other “Buffy” stars also paid tribute to Head, including David Boreanaz.

In a statement to NBC News, the actor who portrayed Angel said, “Anthony was a pillar in the ‘Buffy’ universe and his kindness and sense of humor brought great energy to the set and the first time I met Him. Prayers go out to his family.”
On his Instagram story, he added, “RIP... he was so kind and generous of a soul.”
Emma Caulfield, who played Anya, shared a selfie with Head and reflected on their friendship.

“He was kind and wise and a guide in troubled times. You were so loved. Impossible to caption. You are so missed. rest in peace with your beautiful Sarah,” she wrote in part, mentioning Head’s longtime partner who died last year, according to the BBC.
James Marsters, known as Spike on the show, wrote on his Instagram, “There’s a hole in the World.”

“He was an unflaggingly kind and steady presence on the set of Buffy, and the best actor in the cast. He was the best of us,” he said, in part, adding that he was “lucky to have known, and learned from him.”

Charisma Carpenter, who portrayed Cordelia, also reflected on her time with Head, sharing on Instagram that while she was “not as close to Tony as some of the other cast were,” she’s “still feeling affected by this loss, and my heart is truly with my fellow classmates who remained close to him and his family to this day.”

She extended her condolences to his daughters, whom she hasn’t seen since they were little girls. “But I hear they embody every bit of the love, kindness, and commitment to others that their parents did,” she said.
After recalling meeting Head for the first time and working with him, she also mentioned the “Lie to Me” episode.

“His speech at the end could not feel more relevant today, not only in light of this news, but also given the state of the world,” she wrote. “I invite everyone to revisit it that cares to. Thankfully we can. For that I am grateful. Rest well, Tony. You are loved.”

title: Anthony Head, Who Played Giles in ‘Buffy the Vampire Slayer,’ Dies at 72
summary: Anthony Head, the actor best known for his work on “Buffy the Vampire Slayer” and “Ted Lasso,” has died.
story: Anthony Head, the actor best known for his work on “Buffy the Vampire Slayer” and “Ted Lasso,” has died. He was 72.
“He passed away peacefully of complications due to pneumonia, surrounded by his family,” his daughters Emily and Daisy said in a statement to NBC News.

“It has been, and forever will be, an honour and a privilege to be his daughters, and to have witnessed firsthand the impact both he and his work have had on so many,” they added. “We know how dearly he will be missed by friends, colleagues, and fans of the shows he was in — he loved his job very much, and he always considered himself incredibly lucky, to have been able to work alongside such exceptionally talented people, in such wonderful productions, across a career that spanned several decades.”
Emily and Daisy shared that their grief is “far greater than the hole he has left behind, but we know his legacy will live on, in the shows he was a part of, and in the audiences that love them.”
They concluded by asking for privacy during this time, “How lucky we are to know we are able to watch him doing what he loved, even when he is no longer with us.”
The British actor was most recently known for his work on “Ted Lasso,” playing Rupert Mannion, the nasty ex-husband of AFC Richmond owner Rebecca Welton, portrayed by Hannah Waddingham.

Head appeared in several British shows before breaking through on American television in the 1990s playing Rupert Giles, Buffy’s watcher in “Buffy the Vampire Slayer,” which aired from 1997 until 2003.

Head appeared in 121 of the series’ 144 episodes. One of his signature moments came in a 1997 Season 2 episode entitled “Lie to Me,” in which Buffy (Sarah Michelle Gellar) is feeling beaten down and glum about life.
“Does it ever get easy?” Buffy asks.
“You mean life?” Giles replies.
“Yeah. Does it get easy?” she says.
“What do you want me to say?” he replies.
“Lie to me,” she says.

“Yes, it’s terribly simple,” he says. “The good guys are always stalwart and true. The bad guys are easily distinguished by their pointy horns or black hats. And we always defeat them and save the day. No one ever dies and everybody lives happily ever after.”
“Liar,” she says when he finishes.
Head is the second notable “Buffy” star to die in under a year and a half. In February 2025, Michelle Trachtenberg died at the age of 39 of complications from diabetes.

Head also appeared in such shows as “Doctor Who,” “Dominion,” “Robot Chicken,” “Jack Ryan,” “Highlander,” “NYPD Blue” and “Bridgerton,” as well as the movies “The Iron Lady” and “Ghost Rider: The Spirit of Vengeance.”
His last credit was 2024’s “Upgraded,” according to IMDB.

title: Taylor Swift Paints Ultimate Nostalgia With New Country-Pop Song ‘I Knew It, I Knew You.’ Read the Lyrics
summary: Taylor Swift’s new original single, “I Knew It, I Knew You,” released on Friday, June 5, is a full-circle moment for fans who have grown up with the “Toy Story” franchise and Swift’s stardom
story: Taylor Swift’s new original single, “I Knew It, I Knew You,” released on Friday, June 5, is a full-circle moment for fans who have grown up with the “Toy Story” franchise and Swift’s stardom.

After Easter eggs sparked anticipation across social media, Swift confirmed her involvement in the soundtrack on June 1 and later shared on Instagram her own connection to the movie franchise: A love for the character Jessie.

“Writing this song felt like a musical departure and coming home at the same time,” she said on Instagram to celebrate the song’s release. “Creating something for Jessie was a new challenge and also felt like second nature all at once. And being a @toystory kid from the age of 5 til now… is an adventure I plan to be on, to infinity and beyond.”
Through a series of imagery-filled reflections, “I Knew It, I Knew You” depicts the irreplaceable joy of reconnecting with an estranged friend, romantic partner or something from childhood.
The song starts with not a lavender haze, but a “daze of the blades of the grass in the summer,” in typical wordsmith Swift fashion.
Most of the “Toy Story” movies feature the summer season, from the first movie in 1995 to “Toy Story 4,” where the toys go on a summer road trip.
With a cheery country-meets-electropop sound (the touch of composer Jack Antonoff), plus some emotionally heavy lyrics, the track emulates complex up-and-downs in a relationship that come with time.
“What I thought would be the last time I saw/ my friend/ But love has ways of bringing thinks back to life, Swift sings on the track.

The lyrics are also full of rich imagery. The 14-time Grammy winner has a reputation for color metaphors. In this new track, she sings, “All your blues like a mood ring changing colors,” bringing to mind her iconic chorus led by, “Losing him was blue,” on the title track of 2012’s “Red” album.
For the backbeat, a harmonica leads the way in the instrumentals, leading some to celebrate, as one fan put it, the return of “Taycountry,” fitting for the character of cowgirl Jessie.

Not much is known about the plot of “Toy Story 5,” which hits theaters June 19. The logline previews the beloved cast of characters facing “what kids are obsessed with today... electronics!” However, Joan Cusack, who voices Jessie, has said her character takes center stage in the film.
“This is Jessie’s story,” she told Britain’s Virgin Radio UK on May 28 in a joint interview with co-star Tim Allen. “It’s glorious.”
“This woman comes through,” Allen, who voices Buzz Lightyear, added.
